advisor.advisor_baiqi.desc: General of Qin, known as the "Human Butcher". Never defeated, responsible for the deaths of over a million enemy soldiers.
advisor.advisor_bianque.desc: Legendary physician, pioneered the four diagnostic methods of traditional Chinese medicine.
advisor.advisor_chunyu.desc: Famous physician of the Western Han (anachronistic but in list), kept detailed medical records.
advisor.advisor_fanli.desc: Advisor to King Goujian of Yue, helped destroy Wu. Later became a wealthy merchant known as Tao Zhu Gong.
advisor.advisor_gongsunlong.desc: Famous for the "White Horse is Not a Horse" paradox.
advisor.advisor_guanzhong.desc: Prime Minister of Qi who led the state to hegemony through economic and administrative reforms. "Enrich the country, strengthen the army."
advisor.advisor_guiguzi.desc: The "Sage of Ghost Valley", a legendary teacher of strategy and diplomacy. Mentor to Su Qin and Zhang Yi.
advisor.advisor_hanfei.desc: Synthesizer of Legalist philosophy. Emphasized strict laws and the absolute power of the ruler.
advisor.advisor_huangxie.desc: Lord Chunshen of Chu, served as Prime Minister for 25 years.
advisor.advisor_huishi.desc: A philosopher of the School of Names, famous for his paradoxes and friendship with Zhuangzi.
advisor.advisor_kongzi.desc: Confucius, the most influential philosopher in Chinese history. Emphasized personal and governmental morality, correctness of social relationships, justice, and sincerity.
advisor.advisor_laozi.desc: Founder of Daoism, author of the Tao Te Ching. Advocates for naturalness, simplicity, and spontaneity. "The Way that can be told is not the eternal Way."
advisor.advisor_lianpo.desc: Famous general of Zhao, known for his defensive prowess and the "Thorn Plea" with Lin Xiangru.
advisor.advisor_liezi.desc: A Daoist philosopher who emphasized the impermanence of life and the acceptance of death.
advisor.advisor_likui.desc: Reformer of Wei, author of the "Canon of Laws". Promoted agricultural productivity.
advisor.advisor_limu.desc: General of Zhao who successfully defended against the Xiongnu and Qin. One of the Four Greatest Generals of the Warring States.
advisor.advisor_linxiangru.desc: Minister of Zhao, famous for returning the Jade Disc (Heshibi) intact from Qin.
advisor.advisor_lishi.desc: Chancellor of Qin who implemented standardizations of writing, weights, and measures. Disciple of Xunzi.
advisor.advisor_liuan.desc: King of Huainan (Han dynasty), sponsored the Huainanzi.
advisor.advisor_luban.desc: The patron saint of Chinese builders and carpenters. Famous inventor.
advisor.advisor_lvbuwei.desc: Merchant turned Chancellor of Qin. Sponsored the compilation of the "Lüshi Chunqiu".
advisor.advisor_mengsheng.desc: A Mohist leader known for his unwavering loyalty and martyrdom.
advisor.advisor_mengzi.desc: Mencius, the "Second Sage" of Confucianism. Argued that human nature is inherently good and that rulers must provide for the welfare of the people.
advisor.advisor_mozi.desc: Founder of Mohism, advocating for universal love and opposition to offensive warfare. Skilled engineer and fortification expert.
advisor.advisor_qinhuali.desc: A prominent disciple of Mozi, known for his military skills and defensive tactics.
advisor.advisor_quyuan.desc: Poet and minister of Chu. His suicide in the Miluo River is commemorated by the Dragon Boat Festival.
advisor.advisor_shangyang.desc: Reformer of Qin whose policies laid the foundation for Qin's unification of China. Emphasized agriculture and meritocratic military ranks.
advisor.advisor_shenbuhai.desc: Reformer of Han who focused on "Shu" (administrative technique/statecraft).
advisor.advisor_shijiao.desc: Syncretist philosopher and advisor to Shang Yang.
advisor.advisor_sunbin.desc: Descendant of Sun Tzu and military strategist for Qi. Famous for the "Battle of Maling".
advisor.advisor_sunwu.desc: Sun Tzu, author of "The Art of War". The most famous military strategist in history.
advisor.advisor_suqin.desc: Strategist who formed the "Vertical Alliance" (Hezong) of six states against Qin.
advisor.advisor_tianwen.desc: Lord Mengchang of Qi, known for hosting thousands of guests and retainers.
advisor.advisor_wangjian.desc: General of Qin who conquered Zhao, Yan, and Chu. Known for requesting rewards to prove his lack of political ambition.
advisor.advisor_weiwuji.desc: Lord Xinling of Wei, stole the tally to save Zhao. Respected for his honor.
advisor.advisor_wuqi.desc: General and reformer who served Lu, Wei, and Chu. Never lost a battle. Author of "Wuzi".
advisor.advisor_wuzixu.desc: General of Wu who engineered the capture of the Chu capital to avenge his family.
advisor.advisor_xuhing.desc: Representative of the Agriculturalists, advocated that rulers should farm alongside their people.
advisor.advisor_xunzi.desc: A Confucian philosopher who argued that human nature is inherently bad and must be corrected through ritual and education. Teacher of Han Fei and Li Si.
advisor.advisor_zengzi.desc: Disciple of Confucius, known for his emphasis on filial piety.
advisor.advisor_zhangyi.desc: Strategist who advocated for the "Horizontal Alliance" (Lianheng) to support Qin.
advisor.advisor_zhaosheng.desc: Lord Pingyuan of Zhao, known for his diplomacy during the Siege of Handan.
advisor.advisor_zhuangzi.desc: A pivotal figure in Daoism known for his skeptical philosophy and literary masterpiece, the Zhuangzi. Famous for the "Butterfly Dream".
advisor.advisor_zichan.desc: Statesman of Zheng, known for creating the first written code of laws in China.
advisor.advisor_zisi.desc: Grandson of Confucius and teacher of Mencius. Attributed author of the "Doctrine of the Mean".
advisor.advisor_zixia.desc: Disciple of Confucius known for his extensive knowledge of literature.
advisor.advisor_zouyan.desc: Founder of the School of Yin-Yang and the Five Elements.

tech.jingtian.desc: Ancient well-field land system improves rural organization and yields.
tech.tieli.desc: Iron plows increase farming efficiency and arable productivity.
tech.lunzuo.desc: Crop rotation maintains soil fertility and stabilizes harvests.
tech.shuili.desc: Irrigation expands arable land and reduces drought risk.
tech.dujiangyan.desc: Major irrigation works control floods and irrigate vast farmlands.
tech.zhengguoqu.desc: Large canal project channels water for agriculture and transport.
tech.kanerjing.desc: Underground qanat system provides steady irrigation in arid zones.
tech.tongche.desc: Water wheel lifts water for fields and workshops.
tech.fanche.desc: Chain pumps move water efficiently for irrigation.
tech.yetie.desc: Iron smelting increases metal output for tools and arms.
tech.guangangfa.desc: Co-fusion steelmaking improves material quality and durability.
tech.chaogang.desc: Puddling refines pig iron into more workable steel.
tech.cuihuo.desc: Quenching increases hardness and edge retention of tools.
tech.gufengji.desc: Bellows intensify furnace airflow for hotter, cleaner burns.
tech.shuipai.desc: Water-powered bellows sustain high furnace temperatures.
tech.shichou.desc: Silk production enriches trade and crafts.
tech.fangche.desc: Spinning wheel increases yarn output and consistency.
tech.tihuaji.desc: Jacquard mechanism accelerates complex weave patterns.
tech.ciqi.desc: Porcelain craftsmanship opens high-value artisanal markets.
tech.qingci.desc: Celadon techniques refine glazing and firing quality.
tech.baici.desc: White porcelain perfected with pure clays and fine kilns.
tech.yinshua.desc: Printing spreads knowledge and reduces text costs.
tech.bubing.desc: Infantry drill and equipment form the army’s backbone.
tech.fangzhen.desc: Phalanx formations enhance cohesion and defense.
tech.weiwuzu.desc: Elite Wei troops exemplify discipline and shock action.
tech.qinruishi.desc: Qin elite soldiers emphasize aggressive, decisive tactics.
tech.gongjian.desc: Archery improves ranged capability for field forces.
tech.fugong.desc: Composite bows increase power and effective range.
tech.nu.desc: Crossbows improve penetration and training efficiency.
